name: POLYARTERITIS NODOSA
definition: PERIARTERITIS NODOSA = PAN systemic necrotizing inflammation of medium-sized + small muscular arteries WITHOUT

demographics.Frequency: 4-9÷1,000,000 annually(rare); 70÷1,000,000 annually in patients with hepatitis B
demographics.Mean age: 55 (range, 8-81) years; M÷F = 2÷1 3% of childhood vasculitides in USA
demographics.Path: focal panmural necrotizing vasculitis with nodules along the vessel walls; mucoid
demographics.Histo: polymorphonuclear cell infiltrate in all layers of arterial wall + perivascular tissue

dominant_finding: lobulated renal contour + irregular thinning ← prior cortical infarcts
discriminating_features: multiple small intrarenal microaneurysms (at bifurcation of interlobar / arcuate arteries)
aneurysms may disappear (thrombosis) or appear in new locations

general: multiple small intrarenal microaneurysms (at bifurcation of interlobar / arcuate arteries)
aneurysms may disappear (thrombosis) or appear in new locations
CECT: lobulated renal contour + irregular thinning ← prior cortical infarcts
Chest (involved in 70%): wedge-shaped / round peripheral infiltrates of nonsegmental distribution (14%)
interstitial pneumonitis in lower lung fields
Liver (involved in 50-66%): prolonged washout of contrast material ← increase in peripheral hepatic arterial
Skin (involved in 20%): sulci of high-signal-intensity on FLAIR image
multiple (> 10) aneurysms of small + medium-sized arteries typically at branching points as
aneurysms in small to medium-sized renal arteries (40%)

clinical.Cx: intrarenal / subcapsular / perinephric hemorrhage ← rupture of aneurysm
clinical.Prognosis: clinical course lasts several months to > 1 year; relapse in 40% with median
